I'll pull the Isle of Man life-assurance/portfolio-bond material the cross-jurisdictional map points to.The single most relevant file is `draft` status, so I'll flag that — but it answers the question directly. Freshness is fine (verified 2 days ago).

## Why a UK HNW investor holds an Isle of Man portfolio bond

A portfolio bond is a **single-premium investment wrapped in a life-assurance policy**, written by an IoM-domiciled insurer regulated by the IOMFSA under the Insurance Act 2008. For a UK HNW investor the appeal is the combination of an open investment wrapper plus a specific UK tax treatment:

**1. The UK 5% tax-deferral rule.** This is the central driver. Up to **5% of the original premium can be withdrawn each year free of immediate UK tax for up to 20 years**; the tax is deferred to a "chargeable event" (typically final encashment), and **top-slicing relief** then reduces the effective rate for higher-rate / additional-rate payers. The underlying UK statutory basis is Chapter 9 of Part 4 of ITTOIA 2005. This delivers materially better cash-flow tax treatment than holding the same assets directly — useful for a HNW investor managing income alongside ISAs and pensions. (`knowledge/isle-of-man/life-assurance.md`)

**2. A flexible investment wrapper.** The policyholder (usually via an investment manager) can hold a wide range of underlying assets — funds, equities, fixed income, alternatives — inside the bond, with tax-deferred growth and surrender flexibility. (`knowledge/isle-of-man/life-assurance.md`)

**3. Estate-planning utility.** The life-assurance wrapper offers estate-planning efficiency and is frequently used inside family-wealth structures. (`knowledge/isle-of-man/life-assurance.md`)

**4. Why the Isle of Man specifically.** It is the **world's leading offshore life-assurance domicile**, with a long-established industry (subsidiaries of Quilter, Standard Life International, Canada Life International, Utmost and others), specialist IOMFSA regulatory expertise, and — uniquely among the offshore centres in this corpus — **Solvency II equivalence**, giving its insurers strong regulatory standing. Among the six jurisdictions, the corpus rates IoM as dominant in life assurance, with Jersey and Guernsey only "specialty/smaller." (`knowledge/isle-of-man/life-assurance.md`; `knowledge/CROSS-JURISDICTIONAL-MAP.md`)

**Caveats from the corpus:** watch for product **misclassification** in the policyholder's tax jurisdiction, **adviser-suitability** concerns, and AML exposure through the intermediary chain. Bonds are sold predominantly through FCA-authorised advisers / IFAs rather than direct. (`knowledge/isle-of-man/life-assurance.md`)

> **Status caveat:** the source file `knowledge/isle-of-man/life-assurance.md` is marked **`draft`** — the content is drafted but not yet editorially verified against primary sources. Treat the UK 5%-rule mechanics in particular as needing confirmation against ITTOIA 2005 and current HMRC guidance before acting.
